screens.name: AI Team Supervisor Skill
screens.purpose: Own run routing decisions and dispatch the next agent plus relevant skills.
screens.primary_action: Route the run to the next minimal agent

screens.name: AI Team Operating Manual
screens.purpose: Make future agents choose an isolated worktree and the correct supervisor/website skill stack before editing.
screens.primary_action: Follow task-specific worktree and skill routing rules

screens.name: AI Team Helper Scripts
screens.purpose: Provide deterministic run setup and validation without owning routing judgment.
screens.primary_action: Initialize, refine, print work packages, validate, or print advisory route suggestions

ux_rules: Supervisor routing must be skill-owned, with scripts treated as deterministic helpers.
Keep the website workflow explicit but not blocking for trivial direct answers.
Treat repo design-rule-engine files as the local source of truth before applying external design-skill inspiration.
Name Vercel skills as the preferred implementation guidance for Next.js, shadcn/ui, Geist, v0-style ideation, React quality, and browser verification.
Name Anthropic public design skills as external references unless they are installed into the active Codex environment.
Remove cross-repo installer/template assets that add maintenance cost without supporting current repository work.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Make routing judgment explicit, inspectable, and separated from deterministic helper scripts.
interaction_risks: Agents might let ai:route update run state instead of applying Supervisor skill judgment.
Agents might skip required repo-local skills before routing to Builder.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Dense, scannable operating rules with clear default and exception paths.
interaction_risks: Agents might skip worktree creation if the exception language is too broad.
Agents might treat external Anthropic design skills as installed local tools.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Keep scripts useful for mechanical state handling while preventing script-owned routing decisions.
interaction_risks: Existing callers of ai:route may expect mutation; documentation must say it is advisory.

constraints: Create only operational skill files under skills/ai-team-supervisor.
Do not restore installer scripts or template asset copies.
Define how the Supervisor dispatches agents and sub-skills.
Do not modify product runtime code.
Use onefiveeight-specific worktree paths and codex branch naming.
Keep init, work, validate, and refine helpers available.
Make route advisory instead of mutating run.json.

critical_judgments: Routing judgment lives in ai-team-supervisor skill.
The skill states how agents and sub-skills are dispatched.
Worktree creation is default for non-trivial edits.
Website work uses ai-team-supervisor, website-workflow, and design-rule-engine.

visual_constraints: No rendered product UI changes are part of this task.
Future visual UI tasks must capture browser evidence before and after edits.
